title: trifolium amphianthum
intro: Trifolium amphianthum, also known as the Alpine Clover or Small-flowered Clover, is a perennial legume native to high-altitude mountainous regions in Europe and Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Trifolium amphianthum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and exhibit antiviral activity. The herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
